result:
By default, a child re-renders whenever its parent renders — React doesn't compare props to decide. Common causes and fixes:

1. **No memoization** — wrap the child in `React.memo(Child)` so it only re-renders when props change by shallow comparison.
2. **New object/array/function props each render** — even with `memo`, `<Child onClick={() => ...} data={{x:1}} />` breaks it because the reference changes every render. Stabilize with `useCallback` / `useMemo`.
3. **Context consumers** — any component reading a changed context value re-renders regardless of memo.
4. **Children as props** — passing `children` from the parent usually produces new elements each render; lift expensive subtrees or memoize them.

Quick diagnostic: add `console.log` in the child and use React DevTools Profiler's "Why did this render?" to see which prop changed.

By default, a child re-renders whenever its parent renders — React doesn't compare props to decide. Common causes and fixes:

1. **No memoization** — wrap the child in `React.memo(Child)` so it only re-renders when props change by shallow comparison.
2. **New object/array/function props each render** — even with `memo`, `<Child onClick={() => ...} data={{x:1}} />` breaks it because the reference changes every render. Stabilize with `useCallback` / `useMemo`.
3. **Context consumers** — any component reading a changed context value re-renders regardless of memo.
4. **Children as props** — passing `children` from the parent usually produces new elements each render; lift expensive subtrees or memoize them.

Quick diagnostic: add `console.log` in the child and use React DevTools Profiler's "Why did this render?" to see which prop changed.
